rationale: X is stronger because its conclusion stays closer to the adjusted findings. Y states the aim well and is slightly fuller, but its conclusion claims lower income neighborhoods were associated with excess early mortality even though the reported adjusted mortality signal is Q2 versus Q1, with Q1 only highest in crude mortality. Y also mentions hospital charges without a quantified comparison.
loser_weakness: Y most lacked certainty calibration. Its conclusion overinterprets the mortality result and converts a mixed endpoint pattern into a stronger socioeconomic narrative than the reported adjusted estimates support.
